{
    "success": false,
   "rating": 2,
    "mistakes": [
        "Repeatedly attempted to use python_repl without verifying that the Python environment could import required libraries, leading to persistent ImportError: __import__ not found.",
        "Did not adapt to the environment failure by using alternative methods (e.g., shell commands, basic file inspection) to perform simple EDA or to check/install missing packages.",
        "Spent multiple steps re-reading the CSV file instead of progressing to analysis or modeling after confirming the file existed.",
        "Failed to catch and handle the import error gracefully, causing the agent to stall and not complete the requested pipeline."
    ],
    "lessons": [
        "Before executing code that depends on external libraries, test the environment with a simple import (e.g., 'import sys; print(sys.executable)') and install missing packages via pip if needed.",
        "If python_repl consistently fails, fall back to using command-line tools (e.g., awk, sed, basic Python one-liners that only use built-in modules) or the read_file tool to extract basic statistics and proceed with analysis.",
        "After confirming data availability, move quickly to the next analytical step rather than repeatedly inspecting the same file; use incremental checks to validate each stage.",
        "Implement error handling in the agent's logic: when a tool returns an ImportError, trigger a fallback routine (e.g., attempt to install packages, switch to a different tool, or report the environment issue to the user)."
    ],
    "meta_summary": "The agent verified the existence of test_data.csv and described its columns but could not perform EDA, outlier treatment, encoding, visualization, or regression modeling due to a broken Python import environment. The task remained incomplete."
}
